title: [release-1.43] Backport a number of commits for a security fix
body:
For a recent security fix, backport the following commits into the release-1.43 branch

- https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/fc2003bb2efeb3ed7d5c7e1e88d04e78f370a944
- https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/55c4f2f09eefae3d1a9b7b792790a0deadf2a249
- https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/4a34c646f454c241ac377f299cc752b3299b4672
- https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/8c3eb57bd67191da3e539b11ded23b8549cf7abb
- https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/015c1c73b14a42455c35cf7c0f4e43a3985e7007
 
@Honny1 and @mtrmac PTAL

I had some "fun" backporting this.  If this look OK, I'll add a commit to bump the version too.


<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

#### How to verify it

Uncomment the following comment block and include the issue

Usage: `Fixes #<issue number>`, or `Fixes (paste link of issue)`, or `None`.
-->

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.

@Honny1 @nalind @mheon ugh!  Now I'm getting an error with the new tests that was added for this fix.  I'm not seeing a difference between upstream and this branch for this test, any clues?  It looks like the GIT envars aren't resolving, but I'm not sure why not.

```
[+0664s] not ok 486 bud with ADD with git repository source - .containerignore not applied
[+0664s] # (from function `starthttpd' in file ./helpers.bash, line 116,
[+0664s] #  in test file ./bud.bats, line 7937)
[+0664s] #   `starthttpd /git/=${repodir}:"git http-backend":GIT_HTTP_EXPORT_ALL=1:GIT_PROJECT_ROOT=${repodir} ${repodir}' failed
[+0664s] # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
[+0664s] # # [checking for: docker.io/library/busybox]
[+0664s] # # [restoring from cache: /tmp/bats-run-AkXzj1/suite/buildah-image-cache / docker.io/library/busybox]
[+0664s] # Getting image source signatures
[+0664s] # Copying blob sha256:9758c28807f21c13d05c704821fdd56c0b9574912f9b916c65e1df3e6b8bc572
[+0664s] # Copying config sha256:f0b02e9d092d905d0d87a8455a1ae3e9bb47b4aa3dc125125ca5cd10d6441c9f
[+0664s] # Writing manifest to image destination
[+0664s] # 2026/05/28 15:35:52 listen tcp: address :http-backend:GIT_HTTP_EXPORT_ALL=1:GIT_PROJECT_ROOT=/tmp/buildah_tests.uimdyl/repository-add-git-ignoring-files: too many colons in address
[+0664s] # test http server did not write pid file within timeout
[+0664s] # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
```

This branch is missing the additions to `tests/serve/serve.go` from https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/68b29e64b8a8d03946de5588181736b9d60abc1a.
As a starting point, I would be tempted to try applying just that part with `git show 68b29e6 tests/serve/serve.go | patch -sp1`.
Thanks again @nalind.  I ripped out that test, and have added the line to the test/NEW-IMAGES file.

Ok, in previous runs before I removed a test above it, I see:

```
[+0762s] ok 532 chroot with overlay root # skip expects to already be root
```

Latest run fails and doesn't get the skip?
```
[+0797s] not ok 531 chroot with overlay root
```

Every time we tweak this, a single different test is failing..... Ugh
Do you have pointers to these two results?  The job that's currently failing doesn't fail on that one.

@nalind here's the one that passed but noted a skip: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5048133555585024?logs=integration_test#L980

Here's the one that failed, and did not have a note about skip: https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5717183453462528?logs=integration_test#L1364
Oh, those are different scenarios.  The second hit a flake I've been seeing.

And now with the latest runs, that `chroot with overlay root` test is passing, and we have one new failure:

```
[+0679s] not ok 769 pid-namespace
[+0679s] # (from function `assert' in file ./helpers.bash, line 546,
[+0679s] #  from function `expect_output' in file ./helpers.bash, line 573,
{many lines removed}
[+0679s] # pid:[4026531836]
[+0679s] # time="2026-05-29T10:31:13-05:00" level=error msg="seek /sys/fs/cgroup/system.slice/runc-buildah-buildah3728951415.scope/cgroup.freeze: no such device"
[+0679s] # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
[+0679s] # #|     FAIL: buildah run --cni-config-dir=/tmp/buildah_tests.b5wpan/no-cni-configs --pid=/proc/137795/ns/pid alpine-working-container-2 readlink /proc/self/ns/pid
[+0679s] # #| expected: 'pid:[4026531836]'
[+0679s] # #|   actual: 'pid:[4026531836]'
[+0679s] # #|         > 'time="2026-05-29T10:31:13-05:00" level=error msg="seek /sys/fs/cgroup/system.slice/runc-buildah-buildah3728951415.scope/cgroup.freeze: no such device"'
[+0679s] # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
[+0679s] # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests

```

The pids look fine, I'm not sure how/why it's picking up the time output.
That's a runc flake.  It was fixed, but the VM doesn't have a version with the fix.  Tracked in https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6205
I think we down to packit errors.  Rerunning the tests.
This fetches podman-next packages in packit config and thus a newer buildah-tests than what we need. So, I'd say we ignore the testing-farm failures here. I'll create another PR to fix packit config.
